etymology.description: Originally depicted four ๅนบ(ๅนบ) (yฤo, 'silk') -> semantic ็ณธ(็ณธ) (mรฌ, 'silk; thread') + phonetic ใกญ(jรฌ)
etymology.interpretation: The character ใกญ is an ancient form of ็นผ(็นผ) (jรฌ, 'continue'), where ใกญ itself served as a phonetic element signifying succession, and the character ็ณธ(็ณธ) highlights its semantic link to threads, metaphorically representing continuity.
